hero.title: Joshua Hong
hero.metaLine: Freshman | UTSA Men's Golf | NCAA Division I
hero.definition: High-reliability freshman scorer with proven lineup value and a profile that projects to higher-level competition.
hero.supporting: Low-variance profile with strong counting-round reliability, difficult-condition performance, and early evidence of both scoring floor and contention ceiling.

quickSummary: Immediate freshman lineup value with strong counting reliability and low round-to-round volatility.
Includes an individual win and multiple top-3 and top-10 finishes across the season.
Performs well in difficult scoring environments and holds value when conditions become more demanding.
Already built on real lineup evidence, with clear room for further growth in a stronger daily competitive environment.

metrics.label: Clippd Adjusted Avg
metrics.note: Strong Division I scoring signal with real lineup value.

metrics.label: Raw Scoring Avg
metrics.note: Freshman-season scoring baseline with stable week-to-week value.

metrics.label: Counting Rate
metrics.note: 29 of 30 counting rounds. Strong travel-roster reliability.

metrics.label: Head-to-Head
metrics.note: Strong field-beating record; 75.28% win rate across the full sample.

metrics.label: Top Finishes
metrics.note: Win, podium, and top-10 volume across 10 freshman events.

stages.title: Immediate NCAA Lineup Value
stages.desc: Freshman season translated quickly into win, podium finishes, top-10 volume, and a high-reliability lineup profile.

stages.title: Back-to-Back Mexico Season Champion
stages.desc: Six consecutive wins and a second straight Mexico season title reinforced both finishing power and season-long control.

stages.title: Season Champion Before the Wins
stages.desc: Early proof of cumulative scoring value. The profile first emerged through consistency, stability, and the ability to separate over time.

points: Individual champion and only player under par in the field
Played in a tough scoring environment with clear separation pressure
Strong evidence of difficult-condition scoring value under resistance
Led UTSA to the team title at TPC San Antonio in gusting conditions
Solo 3rd finish with a full-event contention profile
134 (-10) tied the 36-hole school record over 36 holes
Led UTSA to a team runner-up finish, reinforcing scoring ceiling and conversion value
Fall finishes of 9-12-24-55 reflected a normal early NCAA adjustment phase
Spring included a 3-1-3-48-10-63 sequence, highlighted by a win and multiple top finishes
The season already includes 1 win, 3 top-3 finishes, and 5 top-10s in 10 events
The overall profile is still defined more by progression and repeat result volume than by any single week

evidenceProjection.projectedRoleBody: Profiles as an immediate lineup option rather than a long-term development piece, with dependable travel-roster value and counting-round reliability in a stronger competitive environment.
evidenceProjection.translationText: Reliable counting-round addition with value that extends beyond baseline lineup stability.
evidenceProjection.developmentText: Year-by-year scoring improvement and sustained low-round progression suggest room for further growth under stronger daily competition and more demanding weekly fields.

cultureFit: Exceptional work ethic noted by coaches
Comfortable in stronger fields and demanding conditions

transferObjective.body: Pursuing the next competitive step through deeper fields, expanded high-level tournament exposure, and a development environment aligned with long-term professional goals and the PGA U pathway.
transferObjective.noteText: Appreciative of the growth and opportunities provided within the current program, while recognizing that the next stage of development may be better served in a different performance environment.
